name: Storer School Buses
principals: Walter & Gladys Storer (founders, 1952); Warren Storer (2nd gen, special-needs pioneer); current 3rd/4th generation Storer family leadership
fleet_size_est: 200+ (estimate; multi-division CA operator logging ~18M miles/yr across 8+ locations)
employees_est: 500+ (group; Modesto location ~5 per record but division of larger family co.)
service_types: school, special-needs (pioneer), charter, contract shuttle, public transit, casino
districts_or_customers: Modesto City Schools area and Central Valley/Bay Area/Sacramento districts; CA special education programs; multi-location coverage (Modesto, Hayward, San Francisco, Sacramento, Tracy, Livermore, Merced, Santa Rosa, Stockton)
recent_news: 2024 Contractor of the Year (School Bus Fleet); received $3M CEC grant to expand electric school bus fleet with bi-directional charging (2024-2025); UMA Vision Award 2016
fit_notes: Highly attractive Summit tuck-in candidate: multi-generational family-owned (1952) California school-bus and special-needs platform with 8+ locations, EV transition underway, and industry-recognized brand. Record shows tiny Modesto division (5 employees, $366K) but actual group is far larger; verify whether this is the parent HQ entity or a sub-location. Succession could be medium-to-high given 3rd/4th gen ownership.

name: British Bus Co LLC
principals: Janice Brown (owner/principal); Fred Corlett associated historically
service_types: charter (events, weddings, Comic-Con, San Diego Chargers events); double-decker tours; bus sales; NOT a school-bus contractor
districts_or_customers: San Diego-area event/charter clients; no school district contracts identified
recent_news: Yelp shows San Diego location marked CLOSED; Santee LLC still registered (CA entity 200712710119). 2010 incident: Leyland Olympian bus breakdown after Chargers game (San Diego Reader)
website: britishbuscompany.com (domain currently listed for sale on Afternic; Facebook page still active)
fit_notes: NOT A SUMMIT FIT. Misclassified as school bus in the source list. British Bus Co is a vintage British double-decker charter/event operator (weddings, Comic-Con, Chargers games). Domain appears parked/for sale; one location closed. Exclude from tuck-in pipeline.

name: Pleasant Vly Bus Solutions LLC
principals: Paul Skurpski (co-founder, ex-CEO/VP Sales; now VP Sales for GovCon365 at XTIVIA)
service_types: NONE — Pleasant Valley Business Solutions (PVBS) was a government-contracting financial-management software/consulting firm (Microsoft Dynamics, Deltek-adjacent solutions for federal contractors). 'Bus Solutions' in source = 'Business Solutions,' not bus transportation.
districts_or_customers: US federal government contractors (Microsoft Dynamics customers); no school districts
recent_news: Acquired by XTIVIA in March 2018; pvbs.net now redirects to xtivia.com; product line continues as GovCon365
fit_notes: NOT A SUMMIT FIT — MISCLASSIFIED. This is not a school-bus operator at all; it is a federal-government-contracting financial software firm acquired by XTIVIA in 2018. Remove from prospect universe.

name: School Time Trnsprtn-Child Cr
fleet_size_est: very small (likely 1-3 vans/small vehicles; not full-size school buses)
service_types: private school transportation, before/after school care, daycare/preschool, homework assistance, snacks — combined transport + childcare model serving Houston families
districts_or_customers: Houston-area families (private/individual pay model); no school-district contracts; mission emphasizes serving families regardless of ability to pay
recent_news: No notable M&A or news identified
website: schooltransportchildcare.com (site currently displays temporary-unavailability message; Facebook page transportingkids active)
fit_notes: Weak Summit fit. Hybrid daycare/private-pay transport for individual families, not a contract school-bus operator with district business. Too small and outside Summit's contracted-district model. Skip.

name: Charles School Bus Co
owner_or_parent: Independent (operates as Charter School Bus and Transportation Co.)
fleet_size_est: small (likely under 20 buses; serves 4 contracted schools)
service_types: school bus (private and charter schools), charter/event rentals
districts_or_customers: Four contracted charter and private schools in the Durham/Triangle area, NC; field trips and event charters statewide
recent_news: No notable M&A or growth/safety news identified; BBB-listed; incorporated 8/14/2013
fit_notes: Small NC private/charter-school contractor (since 2010, ~4 schools). Independent, owner-operated by William McNeil. Too small to be a meaningful standalone tuck-in but could be a sub-scale bolt-on if Summit pursues NC charter-school niche; otherwise low priority. Note source name 'Charles' appears to be a data-entry truncation/misspelling of 'Charter.'
